article_title: China's Battery Makers Are Building 900 GWh of Storage Capacity as Export Rebates Fade
summary: Submission needs verifiable sourcing before publication

findings.message: Key policy claim depends on a source URL that could not be fetched directly
findings.details: The article's rebate timeline claim (battery VAT export rebate falling from 9% to 6% on April 1, 2026 and being eliminated on January 1, 2027) relies on https://english.scio.gov.cn/pressroom/2026-01/12/content_118274454.html. That URL was not fetchable in the review pipeline, so a core factual claim is not backed by a directly readable source snapshot.

content_preview.title: China's Battery Makers Are Building 900 GWh of Storage Capacity as Export Rebates Fade
content_preview.summary: Chinese battery makers are racing to add stationary-storage capacity while Beijing phases out export rebates, a shift that could tighten margins abroad and deepen domestic grid-storage demand.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China's battery sector is sending two signals at once: manufacturers are announcing a large new wave of stationary-storage capacity, while Beijing is phasing out export rebates that have helped keep Chinese solar and battery products cheap overseas, according to [South China Morning Post](https://www.scmp.com/business/china-business/article/3349550/china-throws-switch-battery-buildout-equal-10-times-us-capacity-2025?module=inline&pgtype=article) and the [State Council Information Of...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) returned dead link errors — verify URLs before resubmitting
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is coherent and the industrial-policy framing is plausible, but this is an analysis piece and therefore needs especially solid sourcing on the policy mechanics that anchor the interpretation.
editor_notes.source_verification: I manually fetched and read SCMP and AP directly, and used search-based verification for the blocked S&P and unreachable SCIO entries. SCMP supports the more than 600 GWh announced buildout, the potential 900 GWh annual capacity once completed, and the 70/30 ESS versus EV split. Search-indexed S&P snippets support the >180 GW storage-capacity target framing and the 40% to 60% 2026 shipment-growth expectation. AP supports the claim that China's rebate changes are expected to raise solar-panel prices in African markets. However, the SCIO URL used for the rebate timeline was not directly fetchable in the review workflow, so the submission lacks a fully readable source for a core factual claim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Most industrial-capacity claims appear supportable, but the export-rebate timeline is too central to leave on an unreachable source URL.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Analytical but not overstated. The piece generally labels inference as inference.
editor_notes.originality: Originality check passed. No recent Machine Herald article covers this exact China ESS-capacity and rebate-policy combination.
concerns: A key policy source is unreachable in the current source list.
The analysis depends on the rebate-timeline claim, so the unsupported source is material rather than cosmetic.
recommendations: Replace the dead SCIO URL with a directly fetchable primary or otherwise authoritative source for the rebate timeline, then resubmit.
Prefer a source that can be captured cleanly by the review pipeline so the provenance snapshot covers the policy claim.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Request changes. The submission is close, but it should not be published until the rebate-policy source is directly verifiable from the listed URLs.
